panel_meeting_name: Health Systems Research Initiative, Research Grants Call 1 - 2014
source_file: government_web_archive/Health Systems Research Initiative Outcomes Call 1 (2014) Call 2 (2015) Call 3 (2016).xlsx
notes:
This scheme is jointly supported by the Department for International Development (DFID), the Economic and Social Research Council (ESRC), the Medical Research Council (MRC) and the Wellcome Trust. This meeting was hosted by the Wellcome Trust and their scoring system of 1-5 was used instead of the MRC's traditional 1-10 scoring system. 

Scoring guidelines 

5* – Outstanding 
4* – Very good
3* – Good
2* – Satisfactory 
1* – Reject 

Proposals are scored by all Board or Panel members after the presentation by designated Board or Panel members and discussion against criteria to reflect their scientific excellence and potential impact, leading to a single median score. At the end of the meeting they are then grouped according to their median scores as a basis for further discussion, which may bring into consideration strategic priorities and portfolio balance. Final funding decisions aim to provide the optimum package of investment.

Median scores of 3 and above are considered fundable, but the funding cut-off is dependent on the budget available at any given meeting. Given very strong competition we are not always able to award scores of 3, and never scores of 2. It is not valid to make comparisons across different meetings.

Applications are listed in numerical order within blocks according to i) their median score groups and ii) their funding outcome. No ranking within blocks should be inferred.

For this meeting, 89/121 applications did not pass an initial outline stage and were not considered further. 11/121 applications were rejected for not being eligible.
